description: Out of the frying pan, straight into the fire.

hints.text: The converse: $(\Box A \land \Box B) \to \Box(A \land B)$. Build the tautology $A \to (B \to (A \land B))$ with empty context, box it with NEC, then use the K axiom twice to chain through $\Box A$ and $\Box B$.
Build the tautology from inside out: assume $A$, then assume $B$, then `(and-intro 1 2)`, then discharge $B$ with `(->-intro B 3)`, then discharge $A$ with `(->-intro A 4)`. Context should be empty.
First K: `(box-elim 6 8)` — from $\Box(A \to (B \to (A \land B)))$ and $\Box A$, get $\Box(B \to (A \land B))$.
Second K: `(box-elim 10 9)` — from the result and $\Box B$, get $\Box(A \land B)$.
